section: operations/ (configuration, deployment, diagnostics, infrastructure, observability, testing, workers)
libraryFilesRead: Baseline data analysis — source verification deferred

tasks.summary: metrics page doesn't exist — 6 metrics classes reference it
tasks.details: 6 broken code-docs links point to operations/observability/metrics: WorkCoordinatorMetrics, TransportMetrics, PerspectiveMetrics, LifecycleMetrics, LifecycleCoordinatorMetrics, DispatcherMetrics. Each class has OpenTelemetry instruments. Need a comprehensive metrics reference page listing all meters, counters, and histograms.

tasks.summary: Diagnostic rule WHIZ300 page doesn't exist
tasks.details: PerspectiveModelConsistencyAnalyzer has <docs> tag to operations/diagnostics/whiz300. Page needs creation documenting this Roslyn analyzer diagnostic.
'length' type has <docs> tag to operations/infrastructure/database-limits. Page about database column/value limits needs creation.

All operations pages have frontmatter but are missing codeReferences. This is the largest gap — each page should link to its relevant source files (configuration classes, observability meters, testing utilities, worker types, etc.).

tasks.summary: LifecycleCoordinatorMetrics documented in lifecycle-coordinator.md but should also be in observability
tasks.details: The lifecycle coordinator metrics table is in fundamentals/lifecycle/lifecycle-coordinator.md but operations/observability/ should have a comprehensive metrics reference covering ALL Whizbang meters. Cross-reference between the two.

tasks.summary: Code samples across all 30 pages have auto-generated poor metadata
tasks.details: Same pattern. ~60+ code samples need metadata review.

sectionSummary: Operations is the largest section (~30 pages). Main issues: (1) metrics page missing — 6 metrics classes reference it, (2) 2 more missing pages (diagnostics/whiz300, infrastructure/database-limits), (3) all 30 pages missing codeReferences, (4) observability coverage needs cross-referencing with lifecycle metrics. No pages missing frontmatter entirely — this section has good structure.
